Increase the temperature of saturated steam without raising its pressure

logical.question: All mammals are warm-blooded animals. Whales are mammals. Dolphins are also mammals. Fish are cold-blooded creatures that live in water. Some fish, like sharks, are very large. Unlike fish, mammals breathe air even when they live in the ocean. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Sharks are warm-blooded because they are large animals.
Whales and dolphins breathe air despite living in the ocean.
All animals that live in water are cold-blooded.
Fish are a type of mammal that evolved to live in water.

logical.question: A company's revenue depends on its sales volume and pricing strategy. If sales volume increases while prices remain constant, revenue will rise. However, if prices are reduced significantly, revenue may fall even if sales volume increases. The relationship between price and demand is complex and varies by product type. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Increasing sales volume always leads to higher revenue.
Reducing prices never increases revenue for any product.
Revenue can fall even when sales volume increases.
A company should always keep prices constant to maximize revenue.

logical.question: Exercise has been shown to improve mental health by reducing symptoms of depression and anxiety. Regular physical activity increases the release of endorphins in the brain. However, excessive exercise can lead to physical injury and mental burnout. Doctors recommend moderate exercise for most adults. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: All forms of depression can be cured by exercise alone.
There are cases in which exercise may have negative effects.
Doctors recommend exercise only for adults with depression.
Endorphins are released only during excessive exercise.

logical.question: In a democratic election, the candidate with the most votes wins. Party A received 45% of votes, Party B received 35%, and Party C received 20%. No candidate received a majority of over 50%. According to the rules, if no candidate receives a majority, a runoff election is held between the top two candidates. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Party A has already won the election outright.
A runoff election will be held between Party A and Party B.
Party C will be included in the runoff election.
The election results will be declared invalid.

logical.question: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level. At higher altitudes, the boiling point of water decreases because atmospheric pressure is lower. Conversely, in a pressure cooker, water boils at a higher temperature because the pressure inside is increased. The boiling point of a liquid is directly related to the pressure exerted on it. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Water always boils at exactly 100 degrees Celsius regardless of location.
Pressure cookers work by reducing the boiling point of water.
At higher altitudes, food cooked in boiling water may require more time.
Atmospheric pressure has no relationship to the properties of water.

logical.question: The human immune system protects the body against pathogens such as bacteria and viruses. Vaccines help the immune system by introducing a weakened or inactive form of a pathogen. This allows the body to build immunity without suffering from the full disease. Not all diseases currently have effective vaccines. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Vaccines cause the full disease before building immunity.
There are some diseases for which no vaccine currently exists.
The immune system only functions after a vaccine is administered.
Bacteria and viruses are the same type of pathogen.

logical.question: Leadership in organizations can be classified as transformational or transactional. Transformational leaders inspire followers by creating a vision and motivating change. Transactional leaders focus on structured tasks, rewards, and penalties. Research shows that transformational leadership is more effective in rapidly changing environments. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Transactional leaders can never be effective in any environment.
Transformational leadership is the only valid form of leadership.
In stable environments, transactional leadership may not be less effective than transformational leadership.
Transformational leaders do not use rewards or penalties under any circumstances.

logical.question: Solar energy is a renewable source of power that relies on sunlight to generate electricity. It produces no direct greenhouse gas emissions during operation. However, manufacturing solar panels requires energy and materials that can have environmental impacts. The efficiency of solar panels varies depending on location and weather conditions. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Solar energy has absolutely no environmental impact whatsoever.
Solar panels perform equally well in all locations around the world.
The production of solar panels may have some environmental costs.
Renewable energy sources always produce greenhouse gases.

logical.question: Sleep is essential for human health and cognitive function. Adults require between 7 and 9 hours of sleep per night for optimal functioning. Chronic sleep deprivation has been linked to increased risk of cardiovascular disease, obesity, and impaired judgment. Some individuals claim to function well on fewer than 7 hours, but research suggests this is rare. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Every adult who sleeps less than 7 hours will develop cardiovascular disease.
Sleep deprivation always leads to obesity in adults.
At least some individuals may require more than 9 hours of sleep nightly.
Most people who believe they function well on little sleep may be mistaken about this.

logical.question: Trade between nations increases when tariffs are reduced. Lower tariffs make imported goods cheaper for consumers. However, domestic industries may suffer if they cannot compete with cheaper imports. Governments must balance the benefits to consumers against the potential harm to local producers when setting trade policy. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Reducing tariffs always benefits a nation's economy without exception.
High tariffs always lead to improved conditions for domestic consumers.
Lowering tariffs can have both positive and negative economic consequences.
Governments should always prioritize domestic producers over consumers.

logical.question: Learning a second language has cognitive benefits including improved memory, better multitasking ability, and delayed onset of dementia. The earlier a person begins learning a second language, the more easily they acquire native-like fluency. Adults can still learn second languages effectively, though the process may differ from childhood language acquisition. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Adults are entirely incapable of learning a second language effectively.
There are cognitive advantages to learning a second language at any age.
Learning a second language is equally easy for children and adults.
Dementia is caused exclusively by the failure to learn a second language.

logical.question: Inflation occurs when the general price level of goods and services rises over time. Central banks typically respond to high inflation by raising interest rates. Higher interest rates make borrowing more expensive, which tends to reduce consumer spending and slow down the economy. However, raising interest rates too aggressively can lead to recession. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Raising interest rates always causes a recession.
Central banks can reduce inflation without any risk to economic growth.
There are situations in which the measures taken to fight inflation can harm the economy.
Inflation is only caused by excessive consumer spending.

logical.question: A school has a policy that students who miss more than 10 classes without a valid excuse will fail the course. Students with documented medical conditions are exempt from this rule. John missed 12 classes due to a family emergency, which does not qualify as a medical condition under the school's policy. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: John will automatically be expelled from the school.
John's absences qualify as medically exempt under school policy.
John is at risk of failing the course under current school policy.
The school's policy treats medical and family emergencies equally.

logical.question: Urban areas generally have higher levels of air pollution than rural areas due to vehicle emissions and industrial activity. Air pollution has been linked to respiratory diseases, heart disease, and premature death. Governments in several countries have introduced emission standards and clean energy initiatives to reduce pollution in cities. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: All people living in urban areas will develop respiratory diseases.
Rural areas have no air pollution of any kind.
Government initiatives to address air pollution exist in at least some countries.
Industrial activity is the sole cause of air pollution in all urban areas.

logical.question: A restaurant's profitability depends on the difference between its revenue and costs. Costs include ingredients, staff wages, rent, and utilities. If a restaurant's revenue consistently falls below its costs, it will eventually close. Successful restaurants find ways to either increase revenue or reduce costs, or both. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: All restaurants that reduce costs will become profitable.
A restaurant with high revenue cannot have financial difficulties.
There are at least two distinct strategies a restaurant can use to improve profitability.
Staff wages are always the largest cost for any restaurant.

logical.question: Antibiotics are used to treat bacterial infections but are ineffective against viral infections. Overuse and misuse of antibiotics has led to the development of antibiotic-resistant bacteria. This resistance poses a significant threat to global public health. Medical organizations urge doctors to prescribe antibiotics only when necessary. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Antibiotics should be prescribed for all types of infections to prevent complications.
Antibiotic resistance is a consequence of how antibiotics have been used.
Viral infections can be cured more effectively with newer types of antibiotics.
Antibiotic resistance is only a concern in developing countries.

logical.question: Online learning platforms have expanded access to education globally. Students in remote areas can now access courses from top universities. However, online learning requires reliable internet access and self-discipline. Studies show that completion rates for online courses are generally lower than for traditional classroom courses. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Online learning is superior to traditional classroom learning in all respects.
Students in remote areas with poor internet cannot benefit from online education.
Online education has both advantages and limitations compared to traditional education.
All students who enroll in online courses will fail to complete them.

logical.question: Biodiversity refers to the variety of life on Earth, including all species of plants, animals, and microorganisms. High biodiversity in an ecosystem makes it more resilient to environmental changes and disturbances. Human activities such as deforestation, pollution, and climate change are reducing biodiversity at an alarming rate. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Ecosystems with low biodiversity are always more stable than those with high biodiversity.
Human activity has had no measurable impact on biodiversity worldwide.
A reduction in biodiversity may make ecosystems more vulnerable to environmental change.
Microorganisms are not considered part of Earth's biodiversity.

logical.question: A country's gross domestic product (GDP) is a measure of the total value of goods and services produced within its borders. GDP growth is often used as an indicator of economic health. However, GDP does not measure income distribution, environmental sustainability, or quality of life. Some economists argue that alternative measures are needed for a complete picture of societal well-being. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: GDP growth always means that all citizens in a country are better off.
A high GDP guarantees an equal distribution of wealth among citizens.
GDP alone may not be sufficient to fully assess a society's overall well-being.
Environmental sustainability is always considered in calculations of GDP.

logical.question: Reading fiction has been shown to increase empathy by allowing readers to experience the perspectives and emotions of characters different from themselves. Studies suggest that frequent fiction readers score higher on tests measuring empathy and social understanding. Non-fiction reading also has benefits but does not produce the same empathy-building effects. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Reading non-fiction has no educational or intellectual benefits.
Fiction and non-fiction reading produce identical effects on empathy.
There are measurable social benefits associated with reading fiction regularly.
People who do not read fiction are incapable of feeling empathy.

logical.question: Public transportation reduces traffic congestion and carbon emissions by moving large numbers of people in fewer vehicles. However, building and maintaining public transportation infrastructure requires significant government investment. In cities where public transport is underfunded, it often becomes unreliable, leading people to prefer private vehicles. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Public transportation is always more efficient than private vehicles in every city.
Insufficient funding for public transport can undermine its effectiveness and usage.
Carbon emissions are not affected by the type of transportation people use.
Government investment in public transport guarantees a reduction in traffic congestion.

logical.question: All members of the committee must vote on proposals before they are passed. The committee has seven members. A proposal requires at least four votes to pass. The committee recently voted on Proposal X. Three members voted in favour and four voted against. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Proposal X was passed by a narrow margin.
The committee will reconsider Proposal X at the next meeting.
Proposal X did not receive enough votes to be passed.
The members who voted against Proposal X were in the minority.

logical.question: Nutrition science indicates that a balanced diet includes appropriate amounts of carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, and minerals. Diets that eliminate entire food groups may lead to nutritional deficiencies over time. However, some people must avoid certain foods due to allergies, intolerances, or medical conditions. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: All restrictive diets will necessarily lead to nutritional deficiencies.
Medical reasons may justify the avoidance of certain foods for some individuals.
Carbohydrates are not a necessary component of any healthy diet.
Vitamins and minerals are more important than carbohydrates and proteins.

logical.question: Technology companies invest heavily in research and development (R&D) to create new products and maintain a competitive advantage. Companies that reduce R&D spending may see short-term cost savings, but risk falling behind competitors in innovation. In fast-moving technology markets, failure to innovate can lead to loss of market share. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: Every technology company that invests in R&D will dominate its market.
Reducing R&D spending always leads to immediate financial failure.
In rapidly evolving markets, lack of innovation may have long-term negative consequences.
R&D investment is equally important in all industries, regardless of pace of change.

logical.question: Crime rates in a city are influenced by many factors including unemployment, education levels, poverty, and law enforcement effectiveness. Studies show that cities with higher unemployment tend to have higher rates of property crime. Improving educational opportunities and reducing poverty are considered long-term strategies for crime reduction. From the information given above, it can be validly concluded that:
options: All individuals who are unemployed will commit property crimes.
Law enforcement is the only effective method of reducing crime.
Crime reduction strategies may need to address social and economic factors.
Education has no proven relationship to crime rates in any city.

If 5 workers can complete a task in 12 days, how many days will it take 3 workers to complete the same task...
A rectangular room is 7.5 m long and 4.8 m wide. What is the area of the room...
The ratio of boys to girls in a class is 3:5. If there are 40 students in total, how many boys are there...
What is 15% of 240...

A water tank holds 1,200 litres. It is currently 35% full. How many litres are needed to fill it completely...
What is the next number in the sequence: 3, 7, 13, 21, 31, ...
A laptop is priced at £720 after a 10% discount. What was the original price...

Maria invests £5,000 at a simple interest rate of 4% per annum. How much interest does she earn in 3 years...
What is the value of x if 4x − 9 = 23...

In a sale, a sofa is reduced by 30% to £595. What is the next price after an additional 10% is taken off the sale price...
A box contains 4 red, 6 blue, and 2 green balls. One ball is picked at random. What is the probability that it is NOT blue...

reasoning.question: Pattern Rule: The shape gains one additional side as you move left to right (triangle → square → pentagon → hexagon → ...). Which shape completes the pattern...
Pattern Rule: A small circle moves clockwise from corner to corner inside a square across each box. If the circle starts at the top-left and moves clockwise, where is it in the 5th box...

Pattern Rule: Each row contains three shapes — a triangle, square, and circle — each in a different shading: white, grey, and black. No shape or shading repeats in the same row or column. In the bottom-right cell, the column has a white circle and grey circle above it. The row has a white triangle and grey square. What belongs in the bottom-right cell...
Pattern Rule: The number of dots inside a shape doubles with each box from left to right (1 → 2 → 4 → 8 → ...). How many dots will be in the 5th box...
Pattern Rule: Each row has shapes that rotate 45° clockwise from one box to the next. An arrow points up in the first box. In what direction does the arrow point in the 4th box...

Pattern Rule: In a 3×3 grid, the shading of each shape gets progressively darker moving from left to right across each row. White → Grey → Black. If the middle row starts with a grey pentagon, what is the shading of the last shape in the middle row...

Pattern Rule: The outer shape stays the same across each row, but the inner shape changes from circle → triangle → square moving left to right. If the outer shape is a hexagon and the first box has a circle inside, what is inside the third box...

Pattern Rule: In each column, the number of sides of the shape increases by one going downward (3 → 4 → 5). The top of a column has a triangle and the middle has a square. What shape is at the bottom of that column...

Pattern Rule: A diagonal line rotates 90° counterclockwise from one figure to the next. If the diagonal line points from bottom-left to top-right in the 1st figure, in which direction does it point in the 3rd figure...
Pattern Rule: The size of the shape decreases from large to medium to small across each row. A row begins with a large circle, followed by a medium circle. What comes next...
Pattern Rule: Each figure contains a shape with a number of sides that equals its row number multiplied by its column number (Row × Column = sides). What shape appears in Row 2, Column 3...

Pattern Rule: Each row contains three shapes. Moving right, one shape is removed and one new shape is added, while one remains constant. Row contains: [Circle, Triangle, Square] → [Triangle, Square, Pentagon] → [..., ..., ...]
Pattern Rule: The shading alternates: every even-numbered box is black and every odd-numbered box is white. What is the shading of the 6th box...

Pattern Rule: Each figure has a shape inside a shape. The outer shape gains one side per column; the inner shape loses one side per row. In Row 1, Column 1: outer = square (4), inner = pentagon (5). What is the inner shape in Row 2, Column 1...
Pattern Rule: In each row, the shape is reflected (mirrored) horizontally from one box to the next. An arrow pointing to the upper-right is in box 1. What direction does the arrow point in box 2...
Pattern Rule: The total number of shapes in each box increases by one from left to right (1 shape → 2 shapes → 3 shapes → ...). How many shapes are in the 5th box...

Pattern Rule: In a 3×3 matrix, each row and column contains exactly one each of: a cross (+), an X, and a circle. No symbol repeats in any row or column. Row 1: [+, X, O]; Row 2: [X, O, ...]. What goes in Row 2, Column 3...

Pattern Rule: A square's corners are progressively filled in — starting from the top-left and moving clockwise — with one additional filled corner per box. In box 3, how many corners of the square are filled...
Pattern Rule: The pattern alternates between two shapes every box: pentagon and hexagon. Pentagons are always shaded; hexagons are always white. What is the shape and shading of the 7th box...

Pattern Rule: In each figure, there is one more line of symmetry than the previous figure (1 → 2 → 3 → ...). The first figure has 1 line of symmetry and the third has 3. Which shape in the 4th figure has 4 lines of symmetry...
Pattern Rule: The shape in each box rotates 90° clockwise and decreases in size with each step. If a large triangle points upward in box 1, which direction does the medium triangle point in box 2...

Pattern Rule: Each row contains: a shape with vertical stripes, a shape with horizontal stripes, and a solid shape — in some order. No shading type repeats in the same column. Column 1 has: vertical stripes (row 1), horizontal stripes (row 2). What shading is in column 1, row 3...

Pattern Rule: The shape's fill pattern changes from white to grey to black in each column going downward. The shape itself changes from circle to square to triangle in each row going right. What is in Row 3, Column 2...

Pattern Rule: A small black dot orbits around the inside of a large square, moving one corner clockwise per figure: top-right → bottom-right → bottom-left → top-left → top-right. The dot is at the bottom-left corner in figure 3. Where is the dot in figure 5...

Pattern Rule: Each figure has an outer shape and an inner shape. Moving across the row: (1) the outer shape rotates 45° clockwise, (2) the inner shape alternates between filled and unfilled. Figure 1 has a white square (outer, straight) with a filled circle (inner). Figure 2 has a white square (outer, 45° rotated = diamond) with an unfilled circle. What does Figure 3 look like...
